settings.dataInfo: Your data is stored locally in your browser. Export your data to back it up, or clear it to start fresh.
settings.clearConfirm: Are you sure? This will delete all locally saved data.
settings.privacyInfo: Vasari Obsidian complies with the Australian Privacy Act 1988 and Australian Privacy Principles (APPs). Your data is processed in Australia (Sydney region) where applicable.

privacyPoints.collect: We only collect data necessary for the service (APP 3)
privacyPoints.use: Your data is used solely for providing toolkit features (APP 6)
privacyPoints.access: You can access, correct, or delete your data at any time (APP 12 & 13)
privacyPoints.security: Data is protected with industry-standard security measures (APP 11)

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Text: Vasari Obsidian ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your personal information in accordance with the Australian Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs). This policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect your personal information.
privacy.s2Intro: We only collect information reasonably necessary for our services:
s2Items: Account information: email address, display name, profile photo (if using Google sign-in)
Usage preferences: theme settings, saved palettes, tint configurations, prompts
We do NOT collect: payment card details (handled by Stripe), location data, device identifiers, or tracking cookies
privacy.s3Intro: Your information is used solely to:
s3Items: Provide and maintain your account and toolkit features
Sync your design configurations across devices
Send essential service notifications (account security, policy changes)
We will NEVER sell your data to third parties or use it for advertising
privacy.s4Text: Your data is stored in Google Cloud Firestore in the australia-southeast1 (Sydney) region. Authentication is handled by Firebase Authentication. We implement:
s4Items: Encryption at rest and in transit (TLS 1.3)
Firestore Security Rules for access control
Regular security audits of our Firebase configuration
No cross-border data transfer without equivalent privacy protections
s5Items: Access your personal information at any time via Settings
Correct your personal information by updating your profile
Export all your data as a JSON file via Settings > Export My Data
Delete your account and all associated data via Settings > Delete Account
Withdraw consent by signing out and deleting your account
s6Items: Firebase/Google Cloud — Authentication and database (Sydney region)
Google Fonts — Font loading (no personal data sent)
Iconify — Icon search API (no personal data sent)
Stripe — Payment processing (PCI DSS compliant, Australian entity)
privacy.s7Text: For privacy inquiries, data access requests, or complaints, contact us at:
privacy.s7Complaint: If you are not satisfied with our response, you may lodge a complaint with the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) at

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Text: By using Vasari Obsidian Web Design Toolkit ("the Service"), you agree to these Terms of Service. The Service is provided by Vasari Obsidian, an Australian-based business. These terms are governed by the laws of Australia.
terms.s2Text: You may create an account using email/password or Google authentication. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. You must be at least 16 years old to create an account.
terms.s3Text: The Service offers a free tier with core features and a Pro tier with additional tools. Pro subscriptions are billed monthly or annually. You may cancel at any time; access continues until the end of the billing period. Refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis in accordance with Australian Consumer Law.
terms.s4Text: Designs, palettes, gradients, and other outputs you create using the Service belong to you. The Service itself, including its code, design, and documentation, is owned by Vasari Obsidian and protected under Australian copyright law.
s5Items: Use the Service for any unlawful purpose
Attempt to gain unauthorised access to other users' accounts
Reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Service
Use automated systems to scrape or extract data from the Service
terms.s6Text: The Service is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, except as required by Australian Consumer Law. To the maximum extent permitted by law, Vasari Obsidian is not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the Service.
terms.s7Text: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or an in-app notification at least 30 days before they take effect.
terms.s8Text: Questions about these Terms? Contact us at
